---
title: "Learning Reflection"
subtitle: "Critical Self-Assessment & Future Goals"
author: "SONGTING CHEN"
date: "2026-04-25"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
## **1. Technical Mastery: Beyond the Spreadsheet**
As an Accounting and Finance student, my traditional focus was on static reporting. This module introduced me to a reproducible workflow:
* Git & Version Control: Moving from bulk uploads to incremental commits showed me how to professionally track progress.
* Quarto Ecosystem: Integrating Python code directly into digital reports ensures data integrity—a critical requirement for modern auditing.
:::

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
## **2. Analytical Innovation with Python**
Using active Python code chunks instead of screenshots was a major step forward. 
* Interactive Viz: Implementing Plotly waterfall charts allowed for a more dynamic exploration of profit erosion compared to traditional Excel charts.
* Code Transparency: Utilizing code-fold allows me to maintain a professional executive summary while keeping the technical methodology accessible.
:::

::: {.callout-important appearance="minimal"}
## **3. Overcoming Deployment Challenges**
The transition to web development was not without hurdles. Configuring the _quarto.yml navigation and managing asset paths required meticulous attention to detail. Successfully deploying this ePortfolio to GitHub Pages demonstrated my ability to troubleshoot technical environments.
:::

## **Future Strategic Vision**
These skills are directly applicable to the modern financial sector. I plan to leverage automated reporting to enhance audit efficiency and use data analytics for strategic SME consulting. This ePortfolio serves as a foundation for my journey as a tech-enabled financial professional.

---
